summary: Possible typosquat of `ulid` / `ulidx` with impersonation signals but no payload
details: Package is published as `ulid-xyz` but its README instructs users to `npm install index-ulid` and links to the upstream `ulid/javascript` GitHub repository, which the publisher does not own. The shipped runtime code appears to be a verbatim copy of the legitimate `ulidx` ULID generator and contains no install/postinstall hooks, no network calls, no obfuscation, and no credential or filesystem reads. However, package.json declares a number of runtime dependencies unrelated to ULID generation (`pino`, `ws`, `postinstall`, `esbuild`, `ts-node`, `tsup`), which npm will pull onto installer machines even though they are not referenced by the shipped JS. The combination of a misleading name, README pointing at an unrelated upstream's install command, and an inflated runtime dependency graph fits the impersonation/namespace-confusion shape, but no concrete installer-harm payload (exfil, dropper, silent-relay, credential distribution) is present in this version. Name-similarity judgments are inherently subjective and benefit from human review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
